text:
There once was a quaint little town where every animal had human traits. There was Mr. Fox who was always late because he read too many interesting books, Miss squirrel who never missed a concert, old Mr. Owl who knew everything there was to know about owls, and little Whiskers who always brought home pet mice.

One rainy afternoon, Whiskers brought home a tiny, lonely mouse named Mousey. Everyone got along amazingly well. Mousey had tales to tell about fancy dances in forest glades.

One Halloween, a big storm came. You wouldn't believe how many tiny mice had dressed up. They were ghosts, fairies, mermaids—every kind of animal you could imagine.

When the storm passed, everyone found little mice homes. Mousey got his cozy little nest upstairs. Every evening, he'd snuggle next to Owl's favorite book—it was actually about owls—but he fell asleep dreaming of tiny mouse symphonies.
